title: Micropus pygmaeus
taxonomy: Micropus pygmaeus is a species of plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in rocky or sandy areas. The plant is typically small in size and produces small, yellow flowers. Micropus pygmaeus is known for its low-growing habit and ability to thrive in dry environments. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Micropus pygmaeus is closely related to other daisy-like plants.
uses: Micropus pygmaeus is a species of flowering plant, however, information on its medicinal uses is not readily available in my knowledge database.
constituents: Micropus pygmaeus contains sesquiterpene lactones, including micropyletin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant also contains other bioactive compounds like flavonoids and triterpenoids. The sesquiterpene lactones in Micropus pygmaeus may contribute to its traditional use as a treatment for various health issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the active constituents and their effects on human health.

intro: Micropus pygmaeus, also known as Dwarf everlasting, is a small, shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, thanks to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Micropus pygmaeus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, this plant is used to make tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health issues, including skin irritations and respiratory problems.
